title: SQL: LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN
content:
### Day 52: SQL: LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN

**LEFT JOIN** — all rows from left table + matches from right (NULL if no match)
**RIGHT JOIN** — all rows from right table + matches from left
**FULL OUTER** — all rows from both tables

**Use Case:** LEFT JOIN is most common — "show all customers, even those with no orders."
codeExample:
# Day 52: SQL: LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN
# SQL: LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER JOIN
sql = """
-- LEFT JOIN: all customers, even without orders
SELECT 
    c.name,
    COALESCE(SUM(o.amount), 0) AS total_spent
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.name;

-- Find customers with NO orders
SELECT c.name
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.id IS NULL;

-- FULL OUTER JOIN (PostgreSQL)
SELECT *
FROM table_a a
FULL OUTER JOIN table_b b ON a.id = b.id;
"""
print(sql)
practiceQuestions: Difference between LEFT and INNER JOIN?
How to find rows with no match using LEFT JOIN?
When to use FULL OUTER JOIN?
keyTakeaways: Review the provided code sandbox to understand execution flow.
